comparisonBoundary: Shared Core architecture responsibilities and dependency direction only; no Shared Core implementation is a migration source.
intentionalDifferences: The Plugin retains Core contracts and canonical-model as separate names instead of Shared Core shared-types; Core contracts own canonical domain DTOs while the separate non-Core execution-contracts package owns cross-host protocol and invocation ports.
The legacy-named Plugin runtime-client is restricted to pure runtime-operation DTO capability metadata; every concrete communication and credential concern remains outside Core.
The Plugin materializer remains a separate deterministic lowering boundary after builder ResourceGraph creation; no generated-final implementation is migrated.
Compatibility differential fixtures remain under compatibility until the test-fixtures package receives sanitized reusable contracts; production packages never import it.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-contracts
packages.finalResponsibility: Canonical application DTOs and deterministic build-result contracts.
packages.rationale: Core contracts contain domain-pure data only; cross-host protocol is isolated in a non-Core package.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-schemas
packages.finalResponsibility: Versioned schemas, manifests, checksums, and provenance.
packages.rationale: Schema ownership is explicit and immutable by version.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-identity
packages.finalResponsibility: String-safe semantic identity, lineage, and IdProvider-facing contracts.
packages.rationale: Identity is not hidden inside canonical-model or package-engine.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-canonical-model
packages.finalResponsibility: Validated application and resource domain projections.
packages.rationale: It consumes contracts, schemas, and identity without replacing them.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-planning
packages.finalResponsibility: Deterministic reviewed-plan parsing, projection, and planning rules.
packages.rationale: The Markdown utility is the first focused parity-proven slice.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-templates
packages.finalResponsibility: Immutable template registry and provenance.
packages.rationale: Template data remains independent from builder execution.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-validators
packages.finalResponsibility: Immutable validation and stable diagnostics.
packages.rationale: Validators never normalize through builder implementation.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-builder
packages.finalResponsibility: Deterministically create ResourceGraph data and provenance from accepted plans.
packages.rationale: Builder returns ResourceGraph and never writes packages.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-materializer
packages.finalResponsibility: Deterministically lower validated canonical structures and ResourceGraph without host orchestration.
packages.rationale: Generated-final materializer migration remains deferred.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-package-engine
packages.finalResponsibility: Deterministic wrapper construction, encoding, decoding, preflight, and artifact metadata.
packages.rationale: Remote transport and package writes remain outside the engine.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-repair-engine
packages.finalResponsibility: Deterministic repair plans and reports over validated engine outputs.
packages.rationale: Repair is a first-class deterministic engine rather than adapter logic.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-runtime-client
packages.finalResponsibility: Pure runtime-operation DTO capability metadata without concrete I/O behavior.
packages.rationale: Concrete communication, credentials, and host behavior remain outside Core.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-runtime-verification
packages.finalResponsibility: Reusable post-deployment assertions, proof contracts, and RuntimeProbe descriptors.
packages.rationale: Browser control remains a Local Runtime or Adapter implementation.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-test-fixtures
packages.finalResponsibility: Sanitized versioned contract inputs and expected outputs.
packages.rationale: Fixtures are test-only and cannot be production dependencies.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core
packages.finalResponsibility: Stable deterministic application facade over approved production Core packages.
packages.rationale: The facade accepts only canonical domain intent and excludes every host execution concern.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-execution-contracts
packages.finalResponsibility: Open protocol-versioned execution context, capability, authority, model-invocation, and result contracts.
packages.rationale: Execution protocol concerns are isolated outside every Core package.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-core-local-runtime
packages.finalResponsibility: Legacy local host lowering and runtime integration outside Core.
packages.rationale: The existing local runtime remains separate from the new host-neutral execution-service incubation.

packages.name: @yeeflow/app-builder-execution-service
packages.finalResponsibility: In-process protocol negotiation, no-write authority enforcement, intent isolation, and deterministic Core invocation.
packages.rationale: The service maps accepted structured intent into a canonical Core DTO and never forwards execution context or model policy to Core.

packages.name: @yeeflow/codex-plugin-adapter
packages.finalResponsibility: Host-controlled model invocation followed by versioned execution-service delegation.
packages.rationale: Host-specific behavior remains outside Core and cannot bypass the service.

packages.name: @yeeflow/web-managed-provider-fake-adapter
packages.finalResponsibility: Deterministic fake managed-model wiring for cross-host equivalence without live provider integration.
packages.rationale: The fake proves adapter isolation without claiming deployment or provider parity.

packages.name: @yeeflow/host-extension-fixture-adapter
packages.finalResponsibility: Open adapter extensibility fixture using the same protocol without a closed host list.
packages.rationale: A new host is added by implementing an adapter rather than editing Core logic.
